report_name: Cloud Provider DataHaven Admits Global Outage Was Caused by Security Breach

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Actor behind DataHaven Breach
threat_actors.description: An unknown threat actor, suspected to be a nation-state or hacktivist group, that breached DataHaven's internal Kubernetes cluster by exploiting a zero-day vulnerability. The actor's goal was the destruction of customer data.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The attackers exploited a previously unknown zero-day vulnerability in an internal, proprietary management API to gain initial access to a critical Kubernetes cluster.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Administration Command
attack_patterns.description: After gaining access to the Kubernetes cluster, the attackers attempted to issue widespread destructive commands via the compromised API to wipe customer data.

attack_patterns.name: Data Destruction
attack_patterns.description: The primary objective of the attacker was to wipe customer data by issuing destructive commands to the storage orchestration layer.

attack_patterns.name: Application or System Exploitation
attack_patterns.description: The attacker's actions, combined with the subsequent defensive lockdown of the control plane, resulted in a service-wide Denial of Service outage for all customers.

vulnerabilities.name: DataHaven Proprietary Management API Zero-Day
vulnerabilities.description: A previously unknown (zero-day) vulnerability in an internal, proprietary management API used by DataHaven. It was exploited by attackers to gain access to a core Kubernetes cluster.

identities.name: DataHaven
identities.description: An enterprise cloud storage provider that suffered a global outage after a security breach where attackers attempted to delete customer data.

infrastructure.name: DataHaven Kubernetes Control Plane
infrastructure.description: A core Kubernetes cluster managing DataHaven's storage orchestration, which was compromised by attackers via a vulnerable API to issue destructive commands.

indicators.name: Destructive DataHaven API Call
indicators.pattern:
title: Destructive DataHaven API Call
id: 2b8a1c3d-f4e5-4b9a-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects attempts to call a potentially destructive API endpoint '/api/v1/storage/delete_all' as seen in the DataHaven breach.
references:
  - https://www.theregister.com/2026/07/01/datahaven_outage_security_breach/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-01
tags:
  - attack.t1485
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1651
level: critical
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri-stem|endswith: '/api/v1/storage/delete_all'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects API calls to the destructive endpoint '/api/v1/storage/delete_all', which was the method used by attackers to attempt data deletion in the DataHaven breach.
